tools.name: eslint
tools.run_command: cd modules/openvino_code && npm ci && npm run lint:all

linting.notes: This is a multi-module repository. Each module has its own linting configuration. The nvidia_plugin uses git-based diff checking for clang-format. Java linting runs in CI via GitHub Actions. Python linting currently has failures (black finds formatting issues in openvino_code/server).

setup_required: Build OpenVINO with contrib modules enabled via -DOPENVINO_EXTRA_MODULES
testing.notes: Testing is per-module and requires OpenVINO to be built first. Tests cannot run standalone. Java API: 'cd modules/java_api && gradle test -Prun_tests -DMODELS_PATH=${TEST_DATA} -Ddevice=CPU'. Custom operations: 'CUSTOM_OP_LIB=<lib_path> python -m pytest modules/custom_operations/tests/run_tests.py'. Token merging: 'pip install modules/token_merging/[tests] && python -m pytest modules/token_merging/tests/'. NVIDIA plugin: requires CUDA hardware and full build. Llama CPP plugin: requires full build and model conversion.

gating_checks.name: Linux - Build and Test
gating_checks.command: Full OpenVINO build with cmake, Java tests with gradle, custom_operations pytest

gating_checks.name: OpenVINO Code - Extension Lint
gating_checks.command: cd modules/openvino_code && npm ci && npm run lint:all

gating_checks.name: Token Merging - Tests
gating_checks.command: pip install modules/token_merging/[tests] && python -m pytest modules/token_merging/tests/

gating_checks.name: Llama CPP Plugin - Build and Test
gating_checks.command: cmake build with OpenVINO + llama_cpp_plugin, run llama_cpp_func_tests and llama_cpp_e2e_tests

advisory_checks.name: Test CUDA - nvidia_plugin
advisory_checks.command: Runs on custom hardware with CUDA, ./modules/nvidia_plugin/utils/check.sh for formatting

ci.notes: This repository uses path-based workflow triggers. Each module has its own workflow that runs only when files in that module change. The main linux.yml workflow builds OpenVINO from scratch with contrib modules enabled, then runs Java and Python tests. NVIDIA plugin tests run on custom runners with CUDA GPUs. Most tests are gating for their respective modules.

build.install_command: Module-specific: npm ci (openvino_code), pip install . (Python modules), OpenVINO must be built first for C++ modules
build.notes: This is NOT a standalone repository. It requires the main OpenVINO repository to be cloned and built with -DOPENVINO_EXTRA_MODULES pointing to this repo's modules directory. Individual modules: openvino_code can be built with npm/webpack; Java API requires OpenVINO build + gradle; Python modules (token_merging) can be installed with pip but need OpenVINO runtime for tests.

conventions.test_naming: Python: test_* functions, unittest.TestCase classes; C++: gtest TEST() macros; Java: JUnit @Test annotations
conventions.notes: Each module follows language conventions. Python uses pytest or unittest. C++ uses gtest. Java uses JUnit with Gradle. Modules are independent with separate conventions.

container_recipe.base_image: node:16 (for openvino_code linting), python:3.11 (for Python linting)
setup_commands: For openvino_code TypeScript linting: npm ci in modules/openvino_code
For Python linting: pip install ruff black

lint_commands.notes: Exit code 1, found 3 files that need reformatting (existing code style violations)

test_commands.command: pip install modules/token_merging/[tests] && python -m pytest modules/token_merging/tests/
test_commands.notes: Requires full OpenVINO installation with runtime module, not available in standard Python container
Requires OpenVINO to be built with custom operations library

container_recipe.test_single_file: N/A - tests require full OpenVINO build
container_recipe.test_single_test: N/A - tests require full OpenVINO build
container_recipe.notes: Only linting can be validated in standard containers. All tests require OpenVINO to be built from source with contrib modules enabled, which is a multi-hour build process requiring the main openvino repository. NVIDIA plugin tests additionally require CUDA hardware. The black linter currently finds formatting violations in the existing code.

validation.summary: Linting: TypeScript/JavaScript (✓), ruff (✓), black (✗ existing violations). Tests: All require full OpenVINO build.

gaps: No standalone test capability - all tests require OpenVINO main repository to be cloned and built
Build process is complex and takes hours (full OpenVINO + contrib modules)
NVIDIA plugin tests require CUDA hardware (not accessible in generic containers)
Llama CPP plugin tests require model conversion and external dependencies
Token merging tests require full OpenVINO runtime, not available via pip
Custom operations tests require compiled C++ library (CUSTOM_OP_LIB)
Java tests require OpenVINO build + testdata repository
Black formatter finds existing violations in openvino_code/server (code style debt)
No unified test command - each module has different test infrastructure
Repository is a collection of independent modules with different build/test requirements
